mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive forward returns do not exceed 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 60% of triggered instances or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand growth that drives corporate earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in more than 50% of cases or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side bottlenecks that compress profit margins and cause rapid negative S&P 500 price adjustments within one week.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns during high inflation periods or produces a positive Sharpe ratio when applied as a short signal.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding 2.5 percent forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which increases discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive in over 55% of high-inflation months or the predictive IC drops below zero in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening that supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over three months.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns in more than 45% of observations or the interaction effect vanishes after controlling for interest rates.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly correlates with corporate revenue expansion, creating immediate positive momentum in S&P 500 prices that persists for at least one trading week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns show no statistical relationship with GDP growth or the signal generates negative alpha after transaction costs.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between industrial production growth and retail sales growth indicates inventory accumulation that precedes corporate earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns within 20 days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth exceeds RSAFS growth by > 1.0%
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative returns or produces positive forward returns in over 60% of triggered instances during late-cycle phases.
